finding: create_main_window moved verbatim from ziee-desktop modules/backend/mod.rs. Verified token-by-token vs the original: the macOS (decorations(false)→Overlay+decorations(true)+traffic_light) branch, the windows/linux (decorations(true)) branch, both Mica/Acrylic/Blur effect vecs with EffectState::Active, the windows post-build create_overlay_titlebar decorum block, and both tracing::info!('Creating main window...' / 'Main window created successfully') strings are byte-identical. Only the 5 literals became config.{title,inner_size,min_inner_size,effect_radius,traffic_light_position}.

spawn_boot_then_window reproduces the start_backend_server spawn skeleton exactly: tauri::async_runtime::spawn → match boot.boot().await → Ok: info!('Backend server started successfully on {}', handle.addr) then on_ready(handle).await then create_main_window; Err: error!('Failed to start backend server: {}', e) (+ verbatim propagate comment) then create_main_window. Both-paths-create-window contract preserved; the success log fires at the same point (before on_ready).

The six domain post-boot steps in the on_ready closure keep the EXACT original order: JWT_SERVICE.set → SERVER_POOL.set → info!('JWT service stored…') → run_desktop_migrations → host_mount::register_provider → ensure_desktop_admin → backfill_system_mcp_assignments → enable_memory_admin_default → state.set_ready(true), then the harness calls create_main_window. No step reordered, added, or dropped.

Every post-boot step's error string is byte-verbatim: 'Failed to run desktop migrations — the app may be unusable…', 'Failed to ensure desktop admin: {}', 'backfill_system_mcp_assignments failed — system MCP servers may not be visible…', 'enable_memory_admin_default failed — Memory will appear disabled…'. All stay `if let Err(e) = … { tracing::error!(…) }` (non-fatal, server stays up) as before.

The moved shell carries NO secret material: create_main_window handles only window geometry/chrome; spawn_boot_then_window passes the BootHandle (addr/pool/jwt) into the app's on_ready closure but the harness itself never reads/logs pool or jwt — it only logs handle.addr (a bound SocketAddr, non-sensitive, same as before). No jwt/pool/token value is logged or exposed by harness code. The auth-carrying mint path (auto_login) stays app-side, untouched.

JWT_SERVICE/SERVER_POOL are stashed from handle.jwt/handle.pool inside the app's on_ready (unchanged from BG-3). The harness receives the BootHandle by value and hands it to on_ready; it does not retain, clone-out, or persist jwt/pool beyond the closure. No new global exposure of credentials; the OnceLock stash surface is identical to BG-3.

Harness names ONLY crate::boot::{BootHandle,ServerBoot} + tauri + tauri_plugin_decorum + std + tracing. Zero `ziee::` code refs (machine-verified grep empty). The reusability contract (harness never depends on the app crate) holds; a second SDK app can call spawn_boot_then_window with its own ServerBoot + WindowConfig.

New pub surface: mod window + re-exports WindowConfig, create_main_window, spawn_boot_then_window. Additive only — no existing harness export (boot::*, manifest::*, single_user::*) changed or removed. ziee-desktop imports the two new idents it needs; no old import broke.

Added tauri = {version='2.8', features=['tray-icon']} + tauri-plugin-decorum='1.1.1', version-matched to ziee-desktop. tauri builds as a plain lib dep (no tauri-build/generate_context! — those stay in the ziee-desktop binary). Machine-verified: cd sdk && cargo check --workspace exit 0 (compiled tauri 2.11.5 + decorum + tray-icon + the harness).

The harness tauri edge was already implied (ziee-desktop→harness existed since BG-3; ziee-desktop already depends on tauri 2.8 + decorum 1.1.1). Adding the same versions to the harness unifies onto ONE tauri build in the single src-app/Cargo.lock (feature-union of defaults+tray-icon). The lock delta is 2 lines (the new harness→tauri/decorum edges), no duplicate tauri.

WindowConfig/create_main_window/spawn_boot_then_window carry no JsonSchema/aide handler and never enter the OpenAPI surface. E8 golden: types.ui.ts + types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.{ui,desktop}.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL to baseline — machine-verified then restored.

spawn_boot_then_window is the sole spawn owner now; it moves boot(Arc<dyn ServerBoot>), app_handle, window, on_ready into one tauri::async_runtime::spawn task, same as the original single spawn. on_ready's Send + 'static bound (and Fut: Send + 'static) matches the original captured-state (BackendState is Arc<Mutex>, Send+Sync; the ziee post-boot ops are all Send). No new task, no new lock, no split ownership.

The per-OS #[cfg(target_os = …)] blocks moved verbatim: macos / windows+linux builder variants, the macos-only Overlay+traffic_light mut block, the windows-only decorum create_overlay_titlebar. On the Linux build host only the linux branch compiles (same as before in the desktop crate); macos/windows arms are cfg-gated out identically. tauri-plugin-decorum is used only under cfg(windows) but listed as a plain dep (compiles as lib on all OSes) — matches ziee-desktop.

run / run_headless / register_desktop_invoke_handler + the 2 IPC commands are deliberately NOT moved: they wrap core::create_desktop_modules + ziee::Config + start_server_with_routes and resolve in-crate #[tauri::command] macros — moving them would force the harness to name ziee::. Verified these files are UNCHANGED by D-full (only backend/mod.rs changed on the ziee side). This is the honest app/domain boundary, not a STOP.

No test moved (create_main_window had none; the boot skeleton had none). The 7 in-source backend tests (ensure_persistent_storage_key_* / create_desktop_config_*) are unchanged and stay app-side (they test config/storage, not the window). Machine-verified: cargo test -p ziee-desktop --lib modules::backend:: — 7 passed, 0 failed.

Seam call shape: `let boot = ZieeServerBoot::new(...)` called via `ServerBoot::boot(&boot)` → `let boot: Arc<dyn ServerBoot> = Arc::new(ZieeServerBoot::new(...))` handed to spawn_boot_then_window. Same dynamic dispatch to the same ZieeServerBoot::boot impl; the Arc lets the harness own the seam for the run. No behaviour change.

The window-close cleanup (.on_window_event → remote_access tunnel stop + ziee::cleanup_server()) stays inline in the app-side run() Tauri builder chain, UNCHANGED. ServerBoot::shutdown (the seam's teardown) remains defined but the live close path still calls ziee::cleanup_server directly (pre-existing BG-3 state) — D-full does not alter shutdown wiring, so no cleanup regression.
